content:
First you complain about jargon and then proceed to use it. Few outside the development community would even know what a mail user agent is. Sure the term is in the RFC, but it has never really been in the popular vernacular.

I have had to take my time to even try and understand it, and I do not. Are you wanting the source so you can compile it yourself, there is no supported build of Thunderbird for raspberry pi. for example. If so see https://developer.thunderbird.net/thunderbird-development/building-thunderbird or is this all a lament for a tarball which is simple to download.

Tarballs have only ever been widely used in the Linux community and are still distributed from the Thunderbird.net website for use on supported operating systems, along with a number of other packages for other package managers such as flatpak and SNAP for Linux and APK for Android. I just went to check in case I just missed something but the tarball was the default download on the site for Linux.

Thunderbird also has a mechanism to locate mail and server settings locally in stored files on installation/setup and has done for all of it's existence in one form or another. Either as predefined accounts/setting or as local files that detect settings for the account.

The auto configuration stuff is documented here https://wiki.mozilla.org/Thunderbird:Autoconfiguration and https://wiki.mozilla.org/Thunderbird:Autoconfiguration:ConfigFileFormat and https://www.bucksch.org/1/projects/thunderbird/autoconfiguration/ by the original architect of the scheme.

There is also provision for admin preconfigured settings to be used See https://enterprise.thunderbird.net/deploy/mcd-thunderbird-autoconfig

So I have tried to answer, but I really do not understand the question.
creator: MattAuSupport

content: When we start the application, all messages are open as tabs. Closing the tabs does not prevent them being open next time we start Thunderbird.
creator: rob.faivre

content: Open messages with a single click in the message pane instead of using the double-click to open it in a new tab. If you prefer to read messages in tabs, close all tabs before you quit Thunderbird: R-click on Mail tab > Close other tabs
creator: Mapenzi

content: Thunderbird on startup automatically displays all tabs which had been left open on closing the previous session. Go to menu Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Open Folder, then quit Thunderbird. In the profile folder search for the session.json file and rename it in session-old.json When you restart Thunderbird a new session.json file will be created and your open tabs from the previous session should have disappeared.
creator: Mapenzi

See https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/getting-started-thunderbird-main-window-supernova#w_5-folder-pane

The Folder Pane is where your accounts and folders will appear. It will show the account name(s) specified in the account settings window, with default account first, and any additional accounts listed in the order you added them. If you are not seeing the Message List Header, click ≡ > View > Layout > Folder Pane.

content:
Hello there serley



It looks like your Thunderbird software isn't displaying your items correctly. Can we try a few troubleshooting steps?




Reset 1. Reset Column Settings:Click the Table View icon in the top-right corner of your message list.Select Apply columns to > Folder and its subfolders > [Your Account Name] to apply default settings.

Repair 2. Repair Folders:Right-click on the damaged folder and choose Properties.Click the Repair Folder button.

3.If these problems occur again, try starting up in troubleshooting mode. If these issues disappear and your settings return, an add-on is interfering with the software.

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content:
I would have thought the text was clear, so I am guessing it is the background you don't have.

Encrypted connections (SSL/TLS mostly) use certificates issued in a chain of trust to encrypt the communications betwen ou and them. Your providers certificate has expired so Thunderbird is not playing. Ring them and advise them to get with it. They have a problem.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Hello there amal



Incoming emails are not being received in thunderbird.okay. What operating system do you use , can i ask? Can we look first at :


Login Open Thunderbird and go to Account Settings > Server Settings.

Imap : 993 ssl/tls security Pop3 : 995 ssl/tls security Smtp port : 485 or 587 with ssl/tls or starttls .

Ensure your server name, port, and security settings match your email provider's exact requirements.Restart Thunderbird in Troubleshoot Mode to see if an add-on or extension is causing the issue.

Check if you are running any active antivirus or VPN services. Log in to your email via your web browser (webmail). If your emails are missing there as well, it is likely a server-side problem.

Did you receive messages for warnings or errors? Can you send me these as well? How much email storage did you have?

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/how-to-make-screenshots
creator: Googlethunderbird.

content: IF the account is IMAP, and IF thunderbird has not run since you did the delete, then you MIGHT be able to recover whatever messages had been accessible when THunderbird was last run. DO NOT start thunderbird as, if this is an IMAP account, the messages will immediately disappear. Instead, do this: - locate the profile folder on the MAC. As a Windows user, I can't advise, but my guess is there is a folder named Thunderbird somewhere. if it helps, the addressbook is named abook.sqlite or abook.mab. A search for that might help in finding the profile. - from there, look in the Imapmail folder for the account and message folders, such as inbox, inbox.msf. - COPY those files and paste into the Mail\Local Folders folder - then restart thunderbird
creator: davidsk

content: Unfortunately, IMAP syncs to the server, not to the PC. Which means that the PC is set to mirror the server. Since there were no messages on the server, messages on PC were removed. Should this happen again, my suggestion would be to exit thunderbird and copy the message folders to the Mail\Local Folders folder. If you have a backup, we can assist in restoring it.
creator: davidsk

content: Outlook offers the option to change shortcut keys. That's in the future for Thunderbird but not yet. With that, my suggestion is to pursue changing the Outlook shortcut.
